human_actions_pending.action: Optional: rotate/truncate %APPDATA%/beekeeper/audit/beekeeper.ndjson (~95MB+ of pre-#13-fix pollen inventory; #13 stops future growth, does not shrink existing)

human_actions_pending.context: user wants strong protection; trade-off is ~12 test updates + blocking npm in gateway/library contexts

decisions.decision: nudge block is detection-INDEPENDENT (does not gate on the pnpm probe)
decisions.rationale: the pnpm probe is ~50% flaky on Windows corepack; a security enforcement control must not fail open on a slow subprocess

decisions.decision: block is the default for INSTALLED protection (installer writes nudge.mode=block) — NOT the code default
decisions.rationale: flipping DefaultNudgeConfig->block cascaded ~12 test failures because npm install is the universal test command; code-default-block would block npm everywhere incl. gateway/library. Installer-writes-block achieves 'protected machines block by default' cleanly. Library default stays soft (PRD 3.2).

a separator inside quotes is literal text (a quoted install never executes), so honoring quotes removes block-mode false-positives on commit messages/echo without weakening detection of real compound installs

decisions.decision: scan audit-append excludes benign pollen `package` inventory (pollenAuditableRecordTypes)
decisions.rationale: ~15k inventory records per scan bloated the audit log to ~95MB and buried forensic records; inventory belongs in scan stdout, not the security audit trail

next_action: Run /gsd-new-milestone to formalize v1.3.0 (Phase 10 + the 5 new commits are its seed), OR /gsd-code-review 10 to review the new commits first. Then resolve the block-as-code-default product decision (task #10).
context_notes: LIVE STATE on this machine: %APPDATA%/beekeeper/config.json = {nudge:{enabled:true,mode:block}}; beekeeper hook installed in ~/.claude/settings.json; binary current at ~/go/bin (Jun 6 19:07, all 5 commits). Block mode is ACTIVE — npm/yarn installs are DENIED by the live PreToolUse hook (proven), pnpm/bun allowed. ARCHITECTURE INTEL: bumblebee=canonical catalog (never exec'd); OSV+Socket=per-request adapters; pollen=local Windows-only scanner beekeeper execs on ALL OSes. Windows state dir=%APPDATA%/beekeeper (NOT ~/.beekeeper). GOTCHAS for whoever resumes: (1) block mode live → a Bash command that quotes '&& <install>' is now fine (quote-aware), but an UNQUOTED compound install in your own command will be DENIED by the hook; git commit -F <file> is the fallback if a commit message ever trips it. (2) The live fail-closed hook locks you out only if `beekeeper check` fails on ~every call — a panic/hang in an always-run path, OR a config the live binary's validator rejects (e.g. mode=block while an old soft/hard-only binary is still the hook). A failed COMPILE is safe (go install won't replace the binary). Order: install the new binary BEFORE the config that depends on it; smoke-test (build-to-temp + pipe a check) only if you touched startup/config/eval code. A bug on SOME inputs blocks only those calls, not a lockout. (3) gsd init.plan-phase/state.begin-phase resolvers map bare phase numbers to ARCHIVED v1.0.0 dirs — pass explicit paths, update STATE/ROADMAP manually. Memory: nudge-block-mode-and-bypass-fix.md, scan-audit-bloat-finding.md, pnpm-windows-detection-timeout.md. v1.1.0 Pollen still PARKED (separate). Everything committed to main, local, never pushed.
